name: pinecone
description: Manage Pinecone vector database indexes and query or upsert vectors

tools.name: List Indexes
tools.description: List all indexes in the Pinecone project

historicalSummary.omitted: index spec details and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Describe Index
tools.description: Get an index's configuration and its data-plane host (needed for Query Vectors and Upsert Vectors)

tools.name: Create Index
tools.description: Create a new serverless or pod-based index

name.description: Index name (1-45 characters, lowercase alphanumeric and hyphens)

dimension.description: Vector dimension (required for dense indexes), e.g. 1536

metric.description: Distance metric: cosine, euclidean, or dotproduct

tools.name: Query Vectors
tools.description: Query an index for the nearest vectors (run Describe Index first to get the index host)

indexHost.description: Index data-plane host from Describe Index, e.g. my-index-abc1234.svc.aped-4627-b74a.pinecone.io

vector.description: Query vector values (must match the index dimension)

topK.description: Number of nearest matches to return

namespace.description: Namespace to query (omit for the default namespace)

includeMetadata.description: Include record metadata in the matches

includeValues.description: Include vector values in the matches

tools.name: Upsert Vectors
tools.description: Write vectors into an index (run Describe Index first to get the index host)

indexHost.description: Index data-plane host from Describe Index, e.g. my-index-abc1234.svc.aped-4627-b74a.pinecone.io

namespace.description: Namespace to write into (omit for the default namespace)

prompts.title: List my indexes
prompts.prompt: List my Pinecone indexes with their dimensions, metrics, and status.

prompts.title: Search an index
prompts.prompt: Describe my Pinecone index to get its host, then query it with an embedding vector and show the top matches with metadata.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Pinecone API Setup

steps.title: Create a Pinecone account
steps.description: Sign up at https://app.pinecone.io — the free Starter plan includes serverless indexes for testing.

steps.title: Create an API key
steps.description: In the Pinecone console, select your project, open API Keys, and create a key (pcsk_...). Copy it immediately.

steps.title: Store the key
steps.description: Add it to your .env file as PINECONE_API_KEY=pcsk_...

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run the List Indexes tool. To read or write vectors, first run Describe Index and use the returned 'host' value as the indexHost parameter for Query Vectors / Upsert Vectors.

notes: Pinecone is two-step: control-plane tools (list/describe/create index) hit api.pinecone.io, while query/upsert hit the per-index host returned by Describe Index
All requests send the X-Pinecone-API-Version header (default 2025-01)
Embeddings must be generated separately (e.g. with OpenAI) — vectors you query or upsert must match the index dimension
